content: The anterograde amnesia framework of *Memento*'s protagonist Leonard can be reframed as a novel self-supervised exploration approach that intentionally discards long-term memory of past successful trajectories to force iterative improvement from only recent failed attempts, just as Leonard repeatedly reinvents his hunt from his limited short-term notes. This counterintuitive setup avoids the common exploration pitfall of overfitting to stale local optima found in past 'successful' trajectories that no longer lead to the actual goal.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
